title: Secret cannot be used more than once in a Containerfile
body:
**Description**
Using the `--secret` option of `buildah bud` does not allow the same secret to be used more than once in a Containerfile

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a simple Containerfile with the following contents:
```
FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret,dst=/home/root/mysecret cat /home/root/mysecret

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret,dst=/home/root/mysecret2 cat /home/root/mysecret2
```
2. Create a `mysecret.dat` text file with some random contents within the same directory
3. Try building the image using: `buildah bud  --secret=id=mysecret,src=./mysecret.dat`. You should get the following error:
```
cat: /home/root/mysecret2: No such file or directory
```
in the 3rd Containerfile step
4. Making a small change to the Containerfile (changing the id of the secret in the 3rd step):
```
FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret,dst=/home/root/mysecret cat /home/root/mysecret

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret2,dst=/home/root/mysecret2 cat /home/root/mysecret2
```
and using `buildah bud  --secret=id=mysecret,src=./mysecret.dat  --secret=id=mysecret2,src=./mysecret.dat` to build, succeeds

**Describe the results you received:**
The secret with a particular id cannot be used more than once

**Describe the results you expected:**
The secret should be used as many times as needed

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
Installed from source

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.24.4
Go Version:      go1.18.1
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.0.1
image Version:   5.19.3
Git Commit:      c1bdaba5
Built:           Fri May 13 15:21:02 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME="Linux Mint"
VERSION="19 (Tara)"
ID=linuxmint
ID_LIKE=ubuntu
PRETTY_NAME="Linux Mint 19"
VERSION_ID="19"
HOME_URL="https://www.linuxmint.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://forums.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://linuxmint-troubleshooting-guide.readthedocs.io/en/latest/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.linuxmint.com/"
VERSION_CODENAME=tara
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux desktopPC 4.15.0-176-generic #185-Ubuntu SMP Tue Mar 29 17:40:04 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**
Empty, but used `crun` as the runtime via `export BUILDAH_RUNTIME=/usr/local/bin/crun`

comments.body: Good Catch! It saves me some time, and I can first switch with other Images, that don't need secrets to buildah.
We had explicit check which was blocking re-using secret artifacts from host again. Above PR should fix it and added a test to confirm that.
Has this fix not been rolled back to Podman? I'm seeing the same issue on v4.5
The fix should be in podman 4.5?  Are you testing on Fedora?
Our build is occurring within `quay.io/podman/stable:v4.5.0`. The first use of the secret is successful, but the second attempt to use it does not find the file.
